title: Maximum Twin Sum of a Linked List

testcases.input: head = [5,4,2,1] Output: 6 Explanation: Nodes 0 and 1 are the twins of nodes 3 and 2, respectively. All have twin sum = 6. There are no other nodes with twins in the linked list. Thus, the maximum twin sum of the linked list is 6.
testcases.expectedOutput: 6 Explanation: Nodes 0 and 1 are the twins of nodes 3 and 2, respectively. All have twin sum = 6. There are no other nodes with twins in the linked list. Thus, the maximum twin sum of the linked list is 6.

testcases.input: head = [4,2,2,3] Output: 7 Explanation: The nodes with twins present in this linked list are: - Node 0 is the twin of node 3 having a twin sum of 4 + 3 = 7. - Node 1 is the twin of node 2 having a twin sum of 2 + 2 = 4. Thus, the maximum twin sum of the linked list is max(7, 4) = 7.
testcases.expectedOutput: 7 Explanation: The nodes with twins present in this linked list are: - Node 0 is the twin of node 3 having a twin sum of 4 + 3 = 7. - Node 1 is the twin of node 2 having a twin sum of 2 + 2 = 4. Thus, the maximum twin sum of the linked list is max(7, 4) = 7.

testcases.input: head = [1,100000] Output: 100001 Explanation: There is only one node with a twin in the linked list having twin sum of 1 + 100000 = 100001.
testcases.expectedOutput: 100001 Explanation: There is only one node with a twin in the linked list having twin sum of 1 + 100000 = 100001.
